name: Salmon/Trout Hollandaise
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut fillets into serving-size portions.
2. Steam over salted water for 9-10 minutes or until fish flakes when tested with a fork. Remove from heat and carefully transfer the fish to a heated platter, keeping it hot.
3. Prepare the sauce: In a double boiler or an oven-proof bowl set over a pan of simmering water, combine the water, crushed peppercorns, and white wine vinegar. Reduce to about 1 tablespoon of liquid. Set aside.
4. Cut butter into pieces and gently soften in a small saucepan. Remove from heat.
5. In the double boiler, whisk together egg yolks, reduced liquid, and some softened butter until creamy and slightly thick.
6. Gradually add more butter in thin streams while continuing to whisk briskly. Stir in lemon juice and salt; season to taste.
7. Remove from heat immediately when desired consistency is reached. If the sauce appears curdling, add a few drops of cold water and whisk quickly for several minutes longer. Note: This method can also be completed using a blender or food processor, though less butter may be absorbed; adding 1 tablespoon of cold water with lemon juice will prevent it from thickening too much.
8. Pour the sauce over the fillets and serve. Serves 6.
